body.u: At sixteen I took seven O’levels and passed six of them. In the sixth form I took three A’levels in geography, maths, and physics, of which maths and physics I passed. When I left school at eighteen I joined an engineering firm as an apprentiseapprentice, and went on a college course to take H.N.D. in engineering, while being paid by the firm. With my H.N.D. I joined a union, and a body of professional engineers, and was earning seven thousand pounds a year. At 23 I bought my first car and moved away from home to take up residence in a flat nearer to the firm. I began thinking seriously about getting married and started to take girls out but I was twenty five before I meet the right girl. She was also intelligent and with both of us working we were earning one hundred and fifty pounds a week between us. We courted for fourteen months and got married in a large church. All the family was there and it was a bit “do”. We settled down in our own house on a housing estate in West London. After being married for two years we had our first child, a boy and we called him William. My wife stopped working and became a full time housewife. Both my wife and I enjoyed an active life and marriage was not going to stop that. We often went camping on the moors and fishing on {illegible} beaches at night. We also went in rally driving races, and we both had a private flying lisancelicence.

Life at work is not to bad and promotion is not to far away, so I am happy at work and not thinking of changing any thing. At thirty two we have another child, this time a girl, and we name her Sue. We are feeling more energetic than ever now and we sell the house and move to a very large cottage in Devon and I get a new job as production engineer in a large engineering works. This job is more interesting because you get out a lot and your job covers larger ground. When I am forty seven my oldest son gets married and we all travel up to London for the weekend. It was a nice week and we get to see all the sights when we were first married. He marries a {illegible} young lady from up North who has just left the W.R.A.F. They settle down in rather the same conditions as we did, only William was a banker and was already in a high position.

After a happy working life I retire, reluctantly, to my cottage in Devon with my, still loving wife. I settle down to a life of fishing in the local streams and digging the garden. My wife continues to do the housework and we still enjoy each othersother's company, even though we are fairly old. At sixty seven we go to a party, as one of my school friends has died when his parachute did not open. He lived for much longer than he expected and as promised left some money for a party on the sad day. Now all my wife and I do is enjoy life until the end.
